title: Dryopteris unidentata
taxonomy: Dryopteris unidentata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris and the order Polypodiales. The kingdom it belongs to is Plantae, and the phylum is Pteridophyta. At the class level, it is classified as Polypodiopsida. It is a member of the division Pteridophyta.
uses: Dryopteris unidentata, commonly known as the scaly male fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, including constipation and diarrhea. Additionally, it has been used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Dryopteris unidentata for medicinal purposes.

intro: Dryopteris unidentata, also known as the Black Wood Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its dark green, leathery fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
